C[matburt: We've worked really hard on it... we just wanted a really simple interface with some great features

Phelan: i mean that other servers have a button that allows you to request an undo to the opponent
matburt: hah... well you are such a better player than me it probably won't matter
Phelan: some people won't play without them
Phelan: others won't play with them
Phelan: it's probably more important when misclicks can happen
Phelan: but with your double click system, it might not be as important

C[Phelan: and definitely not on correspondence
Phelan: but not sure it's even needed, now that i thought about your double click system
Phelan: if people ask for it, maybe
C[matburt: we definitely want to find a middle ground for folks on controversial topics like that
Phelan: but with the system you have, doesn't seem as important

Phelan: that i can see of

matburt: kaya is okay... not crazy about the look and feel personally I wanted something simpler that was more game focused.   Also wanted the whole thing to fit together better, with more features and just generally more cohesion
matburt: Yep... it's those little things :)
matburt: If only my Go playing was up there with my programming abilities

C[Mef: When it was played, G9 felt a little odd (a little too early to make a move this high, something on the 3rd or 4th lines feels more normal like P17, Q14, K4, etc)
Mef: but after the lower right corner now it looks ok, B is one move away from totally sealing that big area in the lower left
Mef: white could have taken advantage by playing a move like K4 for himself.  G7 implies that black wants to make a big area in the lower left, so playing K4 puts a limit on how far black can grow.

Mef: on a tactical note, both M1 and N1 feel a little slow (first line moves are usually saved for endgame).  In either case playing around N4 would be bigger, because that makes a nice tiger's mouth shape for black (so black wants so play it, likewise white wants to stop black from playing it.)

Mef: For a shape note - White O6 makes an empty triangle, usually considered a bad shape because it's inefficient.  Here white is already pretty strong he could even just ignore black's move at N7 to play the 3-3 invasion in the lower left.  That would prevent black from getting all of that huge framework.
